title: Runebound Retrospective - Episode 3 - The Frozen Wastes
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iRyAJEXB_hE

Episode 3 of Daniel's retrospective lands in a landscape of ice and ancient mysteries. The Frozen Wastes expansion features what he considers one of his favorite fantasy game covers—a stunning composition by Jesper Ising that conveys both beauty and peril. But the real treasure here is the backstory: a lonely immortal from the stars, a lost colony ship, and a thousand years of madness that have twisted a mechanical paradise into a frozen hellscape.

The Frozen Wastes shifts Runebound toward planetary romance and sword-and-sorcery roots. New survival mechanics force you to manage frost and white death, seek out legendary items through quest triangulation, and navigate terrain types that punish careless movement. Daniel explores how the weather system escalates tension, and how finding either the princess's crashed ship or confronting the corrupted Arshan creates multiple win paths. The Wike and Onuit peoples, the intelligent wolf companions, and the remnants of Jafar technology all paint a world that feels genuinely alien within Runebound's framework.

----

Does the blend of planetary romance and survival mechanics appeal to you, or do you prefer Runebound's more traditional fantasy roots?
